### S3 — D3: Subprocess / SHA-256 helpers — extract to shared infrastructure module

> T006 extracts spawn_codex, the run_codex_child timeout-poll loop, drain_pipe, tee_stderr_to_file, and the runtime-path builder into a new pub(crate) infrastructure module (codex_subprocess or extending codex_common), making both codex_reviewer.rs and codex_dry_checker.rs call the shared versions. All code stays inside the infrastructure crate (CN-02).
> T007 replaces the inline format!("{hash_bytes:x}") at codex_dry_checker.rs:261 with a call to the existing infrastructure::dry_check::corpus::sha256_hex.
> NOTE — 4-source git-diff union (deferred): The review_v2::diff_getter (GitDiffGetter -> Vec<FilePath>, BTreeSet) and dry_check::diff_getter (GitDryCheckDiffGetter -> Vec<DiffFileHunks>, BTreeMap) implement different ports with fundamentally different output types and line-level vs file-level semantics. Source verification confirms they are port-specific parallel structures (analogous to the lock-acquisition pattern per D3/OQ-2) and are NOT cleanly extractable without creating cross-port coupling. This sub-item is deferred; no task is authored.

- [ ] **T006**: [D3-subprocess] Extract the byte-for-byte duplicated Codex subprocess helpers from infrastructure into a new pub(crate) module infrastructure::codex_subprocess (or extend the existing codex_common module): extract spawn_codex, the run_codex_child timeout-poll loop, drain_pipe, tee_stderr_to_file, and the runtime-path builder (runtime_path / prepare_runtime_path → a single unified helper) from codex_reviewer.rs and codex_dry_checker.rs into the shared module; update both callers to use the shared versions. Respects CN-02: all code stays inside infrastructure.
- [ ] **T007**: [D3-sha256] Replace the inline format!("{hash_bytes:x}") at codex_dry_checker.rs line 261 with a call to the existing infrastructure::dry_check::corpus::sha256_hex, eliminating the semantic dup with corpus fingerprinting. Verify cargo make ci passes.

### S4 — D4: Test helpers — consolidate to single #[cfg(test)] definitions per crate

> T008: CwdGuard / CurrentDirGuard — one #[cfg(test)] definition per test-compilation boundary per crate. infrastructure: 2 copies -> 1 shared test_support definition. cli-composition: commit_hash.rs local copy -> use existing process_guards::CwdGuard canonical (helpers.rs). apps/cli: 2 src unit-test copies -> 1 shared definition; no CwdGuard exists in apps/cli/tests/ so the feasible minimum is 1 total. No cross-crate public API created.
> T009: init_git_repo / init_git_repo_on_track_branch — one #[cfg(test)] definition per test-compilation boundary per crate, with canonicalised git flags (gpgsign=false, --no-gpg-sign). infrastructure: already 1 file, no copies to remove. cli-composition: 2 src unit-test copies -> 1 shared test_support definition. apps/cli: 3 src unit-test copies -> 1 shared definition (src boundary) + 1 existing definition in tests/transition_integration.rs (integration-test boundary) — 2 definitions total is the feasible minimum given the test-crate split, not a DRY violation. No cross-crate public API created.
> T010: stub_binding / StubLayerBindings / NoLayersBindings — single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) usecase test_support module; 3 sets of copies eliminated.
> Each task produces its own commit to keep diffs reviewable. All definitions stay #[cfg(test)] within the appropriate crate — no workspace dev-dependency or public test-support crate is introduced (AC-04/IN-04).

(a) libs/infrastructure: add a single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) CurrentDirGuard in a shared test_support submodule and replace the independent copies in libs/infrastructure/src/git_cli/mod.rs and libs/infrastructure/src/dry_check/diff_getter.rs with imports of the shared definition. (b) apps/cli-composition: the shared canonical already exists as process_guards::CwdGuard in apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/helpers.rs (#[cfg(test)]); migrate the local inline CwdGuard in apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/commit_hash.rs (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) to use that shared canonical instead of its own copy — no other independent CwdGuard struct definition exists in apps/cli-composition. (c) apps/cli — src unit-test boundary only: both CurrentDirGuard copies in apps/cli/src/commands/git.rs (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) and apps/cli/src/commands/review/tests.rs (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) are exclusively within the src unit-test compilation boundary; no CwdGuard usage exists anywhere in apps/cli/tests/; consolidate to a single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) definition (e.g. a test_support module in apps/cli/src/) and update both call sites — the feasible minimum for apps/cli is one definition total. Do NOT create a workspace dev-dependency, a public test-support crate, or any public/feature-gated cross-crate API. References AC-04/IN-04. Verify cargo make ci passes.

(a) libs/infrastructure: both helpers already live in a single file (libs/infrastructure/src/track/render_tests.rs) with no cross-file duplication within the crate; no copies to remove, but canonicalise git flags (commit.gpgsign=false, --no-gpg-sign) in that file's definitions. (b) apps/cli-composition — src unit-test boundary: two independent copies exist — init_git_repo in apps/cli-composition/src/review_v2/commit_hash.rs (#[cfg(test)] mod tests) and init_git_repo in apps/cli-composition/src/track/mod.rs (#[cfg(test)] block); both are in the same src unit-test compilation boundary; consolidate to a single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) definition in a shared test_support module and update both call sites. (c) apps/cli — two separate test-compilation boundaries: (c1) src unit-test boundary: three independent copies of init_git_repo_on_track_branch in apps/cli/src/commands/track/tddd/baseline.rs, apps/cli/src/commands/track/tddd/signals.rs, and apps/cli/src/commands/track/tddd/catalogue_spec_signals.rs (all #[cfg(test)] blocks); consolidate to a single #[cfg(test)] pub(crate) definition in a shared test_support module in apps/cli/src/ and update the three call sites. The per-boundary feasible minimum for apps/cli is one in src + one in tests/: this is NOT a DRY violation, it is the unavoidable consequence of the Rust test-compilation model. apps/cli is explicitly permitted to have up to two init_git_repo definitions (one per boundary). Canonicalise git flags in all retained definitions. Do NOT create a workspace dev-dependency, a public test-support crate, or any public/feature-gated cross-crate API. References AC-04/IN-04. Do NOT introduce cross-layer production dependencies.
